context.projectOverview: Task inbox sprint 4: domain finding INGEST seam. Exported ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) + `bober task ingest [<file>]` (file or stdin). Content-derived stable id = 16-char sha256 of `${domain}|${title}|${kind}` so re-surfacing reconciles to NOOP/UPDATE (one active row, not a duplicate). Validate against imported Finding schema; reject malformed without throwing (exitCode=1).

completedSprints.title: Capture
completedSprints.summary: finding-store.ts writeFinding (routes through writeFact -> reconcile NOOP/UPDATE/ADD) + readFindings (getActiveFacts(HUB_SCOPE,undefined,finding)); task-inbox.ts captureTask; cli task add. FindingSchema runtime + Finding type-only in src/hub/finding.ts.

completedSprints.title: Snooze
completedSprints.summary: isVisibleInDefaultList pure helper; runTaskList now-param; task snooze --until + snooze-until tag.

contract.title: Domain finding intake (pool ingest + dedup)
contract.description: Provide the seam domains write AUTO-surfaced findings through: an exported ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) and a `bober task ingest` CLI that reads a Finding JSON (file or stdin). Ingest assigns/uses a content-derived stable id (hash of domain+title+kind) so a re-surfaced finding reconciles to NOOP/UPDATE rather than duplicating, validates against the Finding schema, and the finding then appears in `bober task list`.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` exits 0 with ingestFinding and the `bober task ingest` subcommand added.
A unit test ingests a watch-kind Finding JSON and asserts it appears in the active hub pool and is returned by the list query.
A unit test ingests two findings with identical domain+title+kind and asserts exactly one active row exists for that id afterward (dedup via reconcile NOOP/UPDATE, not a second ADD).
A unit test feeds malformed JSON / a payload missing required Finding fields and asserts ingest rejects it, the CLI sets exitCode=1, and no row is written and nothing is thrown.
`npm run typecheck` exits 0 — ingestFinding accepts the imported Finding type and returns a typed reconcile action.

nonGoals: Do NOT implement any domain's proactive review pass that generates findings — only the ingest entry point they call.
Do NOT bypass the Finding schema — every ingested payload is validated before persistence.
Do NOT duplicate captureTask's persistence — reuse writeFinding/writeFact.
Do NOT read the clock inside ingestFinding — now is injected.
stopConditions: `npm run build` and `npm run typecheck` exit 0 and the ingest + dedup + rejection unit tests pass.
Ingesting a sample domain Finding makes it visible in `bober task list`, and ingesting it again leaves a single active row.
contract.definitionOfDone: Domain pipelines have one entry point — ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) plus `bober task ingest` (reading a Finding JSON from a file path or stdin) — to push an AUTO-surfaced finding into the unified hub pool. Ingest derives a stable id from domain+title+kind so a repeated finding reconciles to a single active row instead of duplicating, validates the payload against the imported Finding schema, surfaces the finding in `bober task list`, and reports malformed input on stderr with exitCode=1 without throwing.
assumptions: Stable id = a short hash of `${domain}|${title}|${kind}` (mirroring factId in src/state/facts.ts:58-69) so re-surfacing collides on subject and reconciles (reconcile.ts:56-78).
ingestFinding reuses writeFinding (which routes through writeFact) so the reconcile NOOP/UPDATE/ADD logic applies for free.
A finding supplied without an id gets the derived id; a finding with an id keeps it.
contract.generatorNotes: Add ingestFinding(store, finding, {now}) to src/hub/finding-store.ts: validate finding via the imported Finding schema (.parse — throws into a caught boundary), derive id when absent as a 16-char hash of `${domain}|${title}|${kind}` (reuse the createHash('sha256').slice(0,16) approach from factId at src/state/facts.ts:58-69), set surfacedAt=now if absent, then call writeFinding (so reconcile dedups). Return the ReconcileAction. Add a `ingest` subcommand to src/cli/commands/task.ts taking an optional <file> arg (read stdin when omitted), JSON.parse the payload, call ingestFinding, and print the action (added/updated/unchanged). On parse/validation failure: chalk.red message, exitCode=1, return — write nothing. Keep now injected at the boundary.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Verify dedup: ingest the same domain+title+kind twice with an in-memory store and assert getActiveFacts('hub',undefined,'finding') has length 1. Verify validation rejects a payload missing required schema fields and that NO row is written on rejection. Confirm ingestFinding reuses writeFinding/writeFact rather than a raw insertFact. Confirm the derived id is deterministic (same inputs -> same id) and that now is a parameter.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
